title: Populate lyrics for all catalog songs — founder EOD deadline
description:
The founder wants lyrics visible on every song in the app by end of day (2026-06-03).

Scope:
1. For every song in the catalog (functions/src/seed.ts and Firestore), populate the lyrics field
2. Source lyrics for each song — public domain hymns can use full text, CCLI songs should show first verse + chorus with a note about licensing

title: Scope Spotify/Apple Music + song recognition (Shazam-like) integration
description:
Founder wants a Shazam-like song recognition feature integrated with Spotify and Apple Music. The user flow: tap to identify a song playing nearby, match it, pull metadata from Spotify/Apple Music, and add it to the Harmoniq library with one tap.

**Scope this out and produce a plan covering:**

1. **Audio fingerprinting provider** — evaluate ShazamKit (Apple, free, web-compatible?), ACRCloud, and any other viable options. Consider: cost, accuracy, browser Web Audio API compatibility (we're a React+Vite web app, not native), latency, and rate limits.

2. **Spotify Web API integration** — search, track metadata (title, artist, album art, key, tempo, preview URL). Auth flow (client credentials for search, or user OAuth for playback?). Free tier limits.

3. **Apple Music API / MusicKit** — same metadata capabilities. Developer token requirements. How it complements Spotify (some users prefer one over the other).

4. **Web audio recording** — using the browser's MediaRecorder / Web Audio API to capture a short audio clip for fingerprinting. Browser compatibility considerations.

5. **UX flow** — from the song library, a "Shazam" button that: requests mic permission → records ~5s → send

title: Persist album art URLs on all songs
description:
Founder wants album art showing for all songs. Here's the current state:

**What exists:**
- `albumArtUrl` field on the Song type (`src/types/index.ts`)

title: Simplify lyrics pipeline: use lyrics.ovh only, remove CCLI + Musixmatch
description:

title: Integrate CCLI SongSelect + Musixmatch APIs for automatic lyrics
description:
Build the lyrics integration layer for Harmoniq so that songs in the library can have lyrics auto-populated. Two sources, in priority order:

1. **CCLI SongSelect API** — primary source for worship songs. The founder is getting API credentials (client ID + secret). Build the integration so it's ready to plug in:

   - Credentials will come via env vars (e.g. `VITE_CCLI_CLIENT_ID`, `VITE_CCLI_CLIENT_SECRET`) — stub them for now

2. **Musixmatch API** — fallback for non-worship songs. Sign up at developer.musixmatch.com for a free API key:
   - Service module for Musixmatch API (search + get lyrics)
   - Use as fallback when SongSelect doesn't have a match
   - Free tier has limits — cache results in Firestore to avoid repeat calls

The integration should be transparent to users — they add a song, and lyrics appear automatically if available from either source. Manual lyrics entry

- Muted/border tones already exist: `harmonic-border (#DAD6DD)`, `harmonic-muted (#6B6770)`, `harmonic-surface (#EDEAEF)`, `harmonic-background (#F4F2F5)`

title: Build daily catalog expansion pipeline — 1,000 songs/day
description:
The founder wants 1,000 new songs added to the Harmoniq database every day. We currently have ~1,000 songs after recent expansion batches.

Scope:
- Build an automated pipeline (Cloud Function or script) that adds ~1,000 songs per day to the seed catalog and/or directly to Firestore.
- Source from CCLI top-song lists (link-out only, no hosted lyrics for copyrighted works) and public domain hymn databases (full lyrics OK).
- Each song needs proper rights tagging: ccli_required, public_domain, or audit_pending.
- Include genre diversity: contemporary worship, gospel, African gospel, traditional hymns, multilingual entries.
- The pipeline should be idempotent (no duplicates) and runnable daily via cron or Cloud Scheduler.
- Log how many songs were added each run.

Acceptance criteria:
- Pipeline adds ~1,000 new unique songs per execution
- Rights status correctly tagged on every entry
- No duplicate songs (dedup by title+artist)
- Can be triggered daily (manually or scheduled)
- Songs appear in the SongLibrary UI immediately after insertion

title: Phase 1 — AI Translation service (backend LLM drafter)
description:
## Phase 1 — AI Translation service (backend)

Generate draft translations of song lyrics into the choir's target language(s) for human review. Full design: [HARA-28 plan](/HARA/issues/HARA-28#document-plan).

### Model
- Default: Claude 4.5 (Sonnet-class) via the AI gateway.
- Specialized MT (DeepL, Google Translate) is the wrong tool for worship lyrics — they're poetic, not transactional.
- No fine-tune in Phase 1. Build the human-review corpus first.

### Structured prompt includes
- source lyric (line-by-line)
- target language + dialect (`es-MX` vs `es-ES` matters a lot in worship)
- meter constraints (syllable count per line)
- theological register (`Catholic Mass` vs `Pentecostal contemporary` vs `Anglican traditional`)
- optional glossary of theological terms ("Holy Spirit" vs "Spirit", etc.)

### Output
- Line-aligned draft (same number of lines as source) with per-line syllable count.
- Stored under `songs/{songId}/translations/{languageCode}/{version}` with `status: draft` until reviewer approves.

### API
`POST /translations/songs { songId, targetLang, dialect, register, glossary? }` → `{ versionId, draft, status: "draft" }`

### Constraints
- ship `en↔es` first (highes

title: Phase 1 — Song Suggestion AI (backend recommender + LLM re-ranker)
description:
## Phase 1 — Song Suggestion AI (backend)

Build the recommender + LLM re-ranker that powers per-choir, per-service song suggestions on the set-list builder. Full design context: [HARA-28 plan](/HARA/issues/HARA-28#document-plan).

### Architecture
1. **Retrieval (boring + works):** From the choir's library + liturgical priors, pick ~200 candidates and score with a hand-tuned linear/logistic model on:
   - choir usage signal (recency, frequency, success)
   - liturgical / seasonal signal (lectionary date → themes; CCLI seasonal tags)
   - cross-choir popularity priors for cold start (aggregated counts only — no per-choir leakage)
   - service context (position in order, tempo/key continuity)
   - director taste signal (accept/reject history)
2. **Re-rank with an LLM** (Claude Haiku class) on the top ~30. Pass structured features + service context. Output: top-10 ranked song IDs with one-line rationale strings. **Hard constraint:** the LLM can only re-rank from the candidate set — pass an explicit allow-list of song IDs and reject any output containing an ID not on it. Hallucinated song titles are a brand-damaging failure.
3. **Online learning loop:** every accept/reject/replace eve

title: Legal review — Musixmatch translation rights (gates Phase 1 translation feature)
description:
## Legal review — Musixmatch translation rights (gates the Phase 1 translation feature)

The AI translation feature in [HARA-28](/HARA/issues/HARA-28) creates derivative works of song lyrics. Our lyrics are enriched via **Musixmatch**, whose licence covers **display** but is not obviously clear on **machine translation / derivative-work creation**.

Full context: [HARA-28 plan](/HARA/issues/HARA-28#document-plan), section 2.

### What we need answered
1. Does the current Musixmatch agreement permit machine translation of licensed lyrics, with or without human review?
2. If yes — are there reporting / attribution obligations? Per song? Per language? Per translation?
3. If no — what's the upgrade tier or separate agreement that does permit it?
4. Independent of Musixmatch: does CCLI's translation reporting line apply to AI-assisted human-approved translations? (Forward-compatibility question, not blocking.)

### Fallback path if Musixmatch blocks translation
Restrict translation feature to:
- public-domain lyrics (most hymns)
- CCLI-cleared lyrics
- user-uploaded lyrics (where the choir/director attests they have rights)

This is a viable shipping path even if Musixmatch is restricti

title: AI gateway service — unified provider client + audit log + no-training flag enforcement
description:
## Foundation for every AI feature on the roadmap

Build a Cloud Function-based AI gateway service that fronts all provider calls (Anthropic / OpenAI / Google) for the Harmoniq backend.

Full context: [HARA-28 plan](/HARA/issues/HARA-28#document-plan)

### Requirements
- **Unified interface** `aiGateway.callModel({ feature, model, prompt, params, choirId })` callable from any Cloud Function.
- **No-training flag enforced per request** for every provider (not just account-level). Record provider response metadata where available.
- **Audit log** to Firestore: `{ requestId, feature, model, choirId, timestamp, promptHash (sha256), inputTokens, outputTokens, latencyMs, status }`. Do **not** log full prompt content — PII risk.
- **Prompt versioning**: prompts live under `functions/src/ai/prompts/{feature}/v{N}.ts` and the version is recorded in the audit log.
- **Provider abstraction**: Anthropic primary, OpenAI + Google as alternates for the same feature (fallback / A-B).
- **Cost telemetry**: rolled up to `aiUsage/{choirId}/{yyyy-mm}` per feature per choir for billing + budget caps.
- **Rate limiting**: per-choir requests/minute cap, per-feature global cap; fail closed with a clean er

title: smoke
Lock Harmoniq AI Privacy Doctrine as company policy
description:
## Lock the Harmoniq AI Privacy Doctrine as company policy

The privacy constraints in the [HARA-28 plan](/HARA/issues/HARA-28#document-plan) are designed to be company doctrine, not per-feature rules. This issue is the formal lock-in: extract them into a single internal policy doc and reference it from every downstream AI feature ticket.

### Doctrine (verbatim from the roadmap)
1. **No training on customer data without explicit opt-in.** All provider calls enforce no-training / no-retention flags at the request level, not just account level.
2. **No voice biometrics.** Never identify individual singers from audio.
3. **Member data minimization.** AI features get the minimum data needed — no individual attendance records to the suggestion model, no member info to translation, etc.
4. **Per-feature opt-in for sensitive features** (rehearsal recording, practice feedback).
5. **Right to delete.** AI-generated derivatives are deletable on member or owner request, included in account-deletion.
6. **Audit log.** Every provider call logs prompt hash, model id, choir id, feature, timestamp — but NOT full prompt content.
7. **AI output is labeled** ("AI draft — needs review", "Suggested",

title: Add loading feedback to onboarding async actions (RoleSelection, CreateOrJoinChoir, VoicePart)
description:
## Context

## Problem

The three onboarding pages have no loading spinners or disabled-button states during async operations (Firestore writes, auth calls). New users may double-tap or think the app is frozen.

## Files to fix

1. `src/pages/onboarding/RoleSelection.tsx`
2. `src/pages/onboarding/CreateOrJoinChoir.tsx`
3. `src/pages/onboarding/VoicePart.tsx`

## What to do

For each page:
- Add a `loading` state that is set `true` during the async submit
- Disable the submit button while loading (prevent double-tap)
- Show a spinner or loading indicator on the button (use existing patterns from auth pages like `SignIn.tsx`)
- Clear loading on success or error

## Requirements

- Match existing auth page loading patterns
- `npx tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.app.json` must print ZERO errors
- Commit: `fix: add loading states to onboarding async actions`
- Push to main, confirm green build
